Section 1  
Define the function with input and output parameters.  

Section 2  
Initialize a dictionary to store the most common improvement for each age group.  

Section 3  
Iterate through each participant in the input list.  

Section 4  
For each participant, update the dictionary with the most frequent improvement in their age group.  

Section 5  
Return the final dictionary with the most common improvement for each age group.  

* Use a counter to track the frequency of each improvement per age group.  
* Ensure the function handles multiple participants in the same age group.  

Section 6  
Test the function with the provided example input.  

Section 7  
Output the expected result as a dictionary.
